# Test: CODEOWNERS Enforcement Validation (Edge Case)
# Validates that CODEOWNERS file is correctly generated and maintained.
#
# Prerequisites: k3d and Gitea running (handled by TestE2E)
# Environment: GITEA_URL, GITEA_TOKEN, E2E_CLUSTER are injected by test runner

# Step 1: Initialize canonical repo and verify CODEOWNERS
mkdir canonical
cd canonical
exec apx init canonical --org=testorg --repo=codeowners-test --skip-git --non-interactive
exists CODEOWNERS
exec grep '@testorg/api-owners' CODEOWNERS

# Step 2: Initialize different org and verify CODEOWNERS changes
cd ..
mkdir canonical2
cd canonical2
exec apx init canonical --org=mycompany --repo=apis --skip-git --non-interactive
exists CODEOWNERS
exec grep '@mycompany/api-owners' CODEOWNERS

# Step 3: Verify CODEOWNERS content structure
cd ..
cd canonical
exec sh -c 'cat CODEOWNERS'
stdout '@testorg'

cd ..

# Step 4: Initialize app repo - should NOT have CODEOWNERS
mkdir app
cd app
exec git init
exec git config user.name 'E2E Test Bot'
exec git config user.email 'e2e@example.com'
exec apx init app --org=testorg --non-interactive internal/apis/proto/owners/v1
exists apx.yaml
! exists CODEOWNERS

cd ..

# Success: CODEOWNERS enforcement validated
# - Canonical repos have CODEOWNERS with correct org
# - Different orgs produce different CODEOWNERS content
# - App repos do NOT have CODEOWNERS (only canonical repos do)
